framework_alignment: NIMS and National Disaster Recovery Framework; references NFPA 1600, EMAP, FEMA CPG 101/201, SUNY ERP Requirements
published_revised: Full plan review and update to version 3.0, 3/25/2020
evidence_quote: This Continuity of Operations Plan provides the framework by which Cornell University will mitigate impacts and restore essential services and operations at the University, College, or Division level. This plan identifies the overall concepts to manage University continuity objectives, identify operational priorities, clarify continuity of leadership, and activate processes and procedures for use during continuity operations.
catalog_notes: University-wide COOP organized around continuity guiding principles and Emergency Support Functions. Supported by C-COOP web tool. Activates within 12 hours, sustains up to 30 days. Includes university order of succession, vital records strategy, academic and research continuity, continuity locations.

framework_alignment: Business Continuity Management Process (pre-incident planning, incident response, plan development)
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Plan is the process whereby organizations ensure the maintenance of critical operations when confronted with adverse events such as natural disasters, technology failures, human errors, or terrorism. The objectives of a business continuity plan are to minimize loss of the organization, continue to serve customers, and maintain administrative operations.
catalog_notes: University-wide BCP with detailed Business Impact Analysis tables by division (allowable downtime and recovery priority), hazard matrix/risk assessment, and unit-plan testing/maintenance requirements (exercised at least every two years). DIT maintains a separate Disaster Recovery Plan.

framework_alignment: FEMA four-phase continuity model (Readiness/Preparedness; Activation/Relocation; Continuity Operations; Reconstitution)
evidence_quote: This Continuity of Operations (COOP) Plan provides the overarching program management framework for the University of Maryland, Baltimore (UMB) Continuity Program. It identifies how the Office of Emergency Management (OEM) supports offices and schools (herein known as “departments”) plan and prepare to continue performing the most essential work despite interruptions and impacts to, “people, places, and things”.
catalog_notes: Program-management framework built around People/Places/Things and four continuity phases. Uses Veoci for plan maintenance; CORE departments revise every two years. Health-sciences-focused campus (medicine, nursing, law, dentistry, pharmacy, social work). Includes Alternate Space Requirements Tool and Essential Program Functions list.

published_revised: Rebranded COOPs to Mission Continuity Plans in 2023
evidence_quote: A Mission Continuity Plan documents how the division or department will perform essential operations during an emergency situation or long-term disruption, which might last from 2 days to several weeks. The plan will identify mission-critical functions, departmental communication methods, and alternate personnel, systems and locations.
catalog_notes: Princeton rebranded its Continuity of Operations Plans as Mission Continuity Plans in 2023. EHS manages a central secure repository of all division Mission Continuity Plans. Program page describes planning principles focused on essential functions, alternate decision-makers, and resource loss scenarios.

institution_name: Rutgers, The State University of New Jersey
framework_alignment: 10 Phases to Business Continuity Planning (DRI/BCM professional practices)
evidence_quote: Rutgers University, along with its officials, schools, units, departments, offices, and other internal organizations, is preparing a comprehensive and effective Continuity of Operations/Continuity of Governance Plan to ensure that essential operations can be performed during an emergency situation that may disrupt normal operations.
catalog_notes: RU Ready program page describes a Continuity of Operations/Continuity of Governance Plan, the 10 phases of business continuity planning, and Business Impact Analysis objectives including recovery objectives and timelines. Largest university in New Jersey.

evidence_quote: The goal of academic and business continuity planning is to enable the University of Pittsburgh to maintain operations and services in the face of a disruptive or catastrophic event. The longer an institution takes to recover, the greater damage it sustains to its reputation. At Pitt, we call this plan UPitt Ready, which is a web-based continuity planning tool to create useful, thoughtful plans to ensure each unit's ability to continue its critical functions if an incident occurs.
catalog_notes: UPitt Ready is Pitt's academic and business continuity planning system built in Kuali. Methodology avoids scenario-specific planning in favor of identifying critical functions and resources. Units appoint a Plan Manager; plans reviewed annually.

evidence_quote: A Business Continuity Plan (BCP) is a collection of resources, actions, procedures, and information that is developed, tested, and held in readiness for use in the event of a disaster or major disruption of operations. A BCP helps prepare Stony Brook departments and organizations to maintain essential functions after a disaster or disruption.
catalog_notes: SUNY flagship R1. Business continuity program managed by the Division of Enterprise Risk Management / Emergency Management, with departmental plans developed in Veoci. Emphasis on laboratory/research continuity.

framework_alignment: Six-stage continuity planning lifecycle (assessment, strategy, plan creation, training, testing, maintenance)
evidence_quote: At Temple University, we recognize the importance of continuity planning to ensure that we can maintain our critical functions, regardless of unforeseen circumstances.
catalog_notes: Office of Emergency Management runs a decentralized continuity program covering education, research, facilities, financial health, and clinical services. Six stages: assessment/analysis, strategy development, plan creation, training/awareness, testing/exercises, maintenance/review.

framework_alignment: University System of Maryland COOP requirement
evidence_quote: Continuity of Operations (COOP), also referred to as business continuity, is the ability to continue to perform critical activities when normal operating conditions are disrupted (e.g., key staff are ill, the office loses power). The University System of Maryland (USM) requires COOP plans to ensure its institutions can maintain their critical functions when faced with unexpected challenges.
catalog_notes: Office of Emergency Management and Business Continuity (OEMBC) administers the COOP program; created a department COOP template centered on critical activities. Campus EOP (2022) is the foundation. USM mandates COOP plans.

framework_alignment: USM Policy VI-13.00; State of Maryland COOP Guide; essential-functions hierarchy (SEF/IEF/UEF/PMEF/MEF/ESA/MEA)
evidence_quote: This COOP instructs each College, Division, and subordinate unit on the process for identifying essential functions. It describes the levels of essential functions, identifies the University’s Essential Functions, and states how they align with Institutional Essential Functions from USM and State Essential Functions from the State of Maryland.
catalog_notes: Exceptionally detailed 30-page COOP as Annex 3 of the Base EOP. Four-phase continuity process (identify MEFs, BPA, BIA, prioritize/document); defines MTD, RTO, RPO; aligns with USM VI-13.00 and Maryland State COOP Guide. Includes Continuity Working Group, DEAPs, and MEF/BPA data sheet templates. Marked For Official Use Only but publicly posted.

framework_alignment: References FEMA IS-1300 Introduction to Continuity
evidence_quote: Department plans should be prepared with the goal of enabling the University's essential functions to continue regardless of whether or not systems are operational, facilities and infrastructure services are available, or other organizations are viable.
catalog_notes: Institution-published departmental Business Continuity Plan template (Jesuit university). Captures emergency contacts, office equipment, critical/essential operations in order of importance, readiness checklist, essential resources/supplies/services, and chair/director sign-off. Stored in SharePoint; all campus departments required to complete a BCP.

framework_alignment: Three-component model (policy/procedure; risk assessment and BIA; testing/review)
evidence_quote: A business continuity plan (BCP) is a collection of resources, actions, procedures, and information that is developed, tested, and held in readiness for use in the event of a disaster or major disruption of operations. The objective of the Business Continuity Plan is to establish policies, procedures, and coordinate recovery of critical University functions.
catalog_notes: Detailed BCP framework page plus a downloadable Departmental Business Continuity Plan Template (DOCX). Defines five priority/recovery-time tiers (Critical <4 hours through Deferable >1 month), four loss scenarios, BIA, and crisis communication. NYC and Westchester campuses.

plan_type: Continuity of Instruction / Academic Continuity Plan
evidence_quote: To ensure academic continuity, Montclair State faculty are encouraged to adapt their instruction to allow for flexibility. Developing your course with flexibility in mind will help to make your course more resilient to ensure interruptions do not impede teaching.
catalog_notes: Institution-published academic/instructional continuity guidance from Instructional Technology and Design Services. Focuses on resilient course design, Canvas announcements, syllabus continuity statements, online activities, and accessibility (UDL) rather than enterprise COOP.

framework_alignment: SUNY COOP statewide initiative; 12-hour/30-day continuity standard
evidence_quote: The Binghamton University Continuity Planning Tool (B-READY) is a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet designed to allow each campus office/department/division to develop a Continuity of Operation Plan (COOP). The tool is designed as an effort within individual departments to ensure continuity of their essential functions across a wide range of emergencies and events.
catalog_notes: B-READY is Binghamton's published COOP template tool (downloadable spreadsheet) supporting the SUNY statewide COOP initiative. Goals include COOP operational within 12 hours, sustained up to 30 days, regular testing/training/exercising, and risk analysis of alternate operating facilities.

framework_alignment: Business Continuity Life Cycle (all-hazards, four loss scenarios); Fusion Framework platform
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Planning documents the actions and activities that a business function will execute to establish and sustain continuity of operations at an acceptable level within recovery objective(s) in the event of a disaster or significant business disruption. Plans are designed on an all-hazard approach focusing on four key loss scenarios: Loss of Facility, Loss of People, Loss of Supplier, Loss of Technology.
catalog_notes: Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity Services (Enterprise Risk Management) runs a full BC Life Cycle: BIA, Risk Assessment and Gap Analysis, Business Continuity Planning, and Plan Exercising (walkthrough/tabletop/functional). Plans maintained in Fusion Framework; recovery objectives defined.

evidence_quote: Continuity planning involves identifying your unit’s Critical functions, Key resources, Key decision makers, Information technology tools and building a plan for how you would protect those functions in the event of a disruption.
catalog_notes: Office of Emergency Management runs UD's COOP program and maintains the University Emergency Management/COOP Policy. Program page cites recent disruptions (Winter Storm Fern 2026, COVID-19) and links to the general counsel Emergency Management Policy. Department COOP plans drafted via OEM-supported form.

evidence_quote: Business continuity planning is the process of developing prior arrangements and procedures that enable Syracuse University to respond to a disaster or major disruption of operations in such a manner that critical and essential business functions can continue with minimum disruption or down time.
catalog_notes: Department of Public Safety Emergency Management runs the BC program with a Business Continuity Specialist; departmental plans developed and tested for use after a disaster or major disruption.

framework_alignment: Risk-based BC/DR policy tied to Columbia University Information Security Charter
evidence_quote: Each Executive Manager is required to perform a Business Risk Assessment and Business Impact Analysis for each Key Business System that is used in his/her area of responsibility. The assessment should identify and define the criticality of Key Business Systems and the repositories that contain the relevant and necessary University Data for the Key Business System.
catalog_notes: University-wide Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Policy defining acceptable methods for BC/DR planning via risk-based analysis. Requires Business Impact Analysis, prioritized recovery, Recovery Time Objectives and Recovery Point Objectives, contingency plans (emergency mode operations + recovery), and data backup plans with periodic testing.

framework_alignment: CUNY Risk Management and Business Continuity (RMBC) Council guidelines
evidence_quote: Business Continuity planning (also called Continuity of Operations planning) is a concept that evolves out of Risk Management as a type of Risk Mitigation Control. Like Emergency Management, Business Continuity planning is a large enough task to be considered as a separate set of processes, policies and procedures.
catalog_notes: CUNY senior college and Hispanic-Serving Institution. Risk Management/Emergency Management page describes departmental-level BC planning under CUNY RMBC Council guidelines (2013 draft), referencing 9/11, Irene, and Sandy. CUNY IT Disaster Recovery Plan developed 2013-2014.

evidence_quote: Users managing University's IT Resources must have documented Business Continuity (BC) and Disaster Recovery (DR) plans for Fordham-managed IT Resources.
catalog_notes: Jesuit university. IT-focused Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery Policy requiring documented BC/DR plans, geographically diverse plan storage, annual review, and DR simulation/tabletop and recovery tests. Owned by Senior Director of IT Security and Assurance; approved by CISO.

evidence_quote: Howard University must ensure its operations are performed efficiently with minimal disruption through a wide range of emergencies. The Continuity of Operations Planning Program ensures that the organization is capable of conducting its essential mission and functions under all threats and conditions.
catalog_notes: Leading private HBCU. Department of Public Safety Office of Emergency Management runs the COOP program and publishes the all-hazards Howard University Emergency Management Plan (HU EMP) as a PDF on the official host. Plans page describes COOP alongside the EMP framework.

framework_alignment: Kuali-based business continuity program; EOP covers prevention/protection/mitigation/response/recovery
published_revised: Drexel University Emergency Operations Plan, Last Revised March 2021
evidence_quote: Business Continuity Plans are organized and maintained on the Kuali platform administered by Public Safety. These plans can be accessed by internal stakeholders through the DrexelReady online administrator portal (Kuali).
catalog_notes: DrexelReady institutional preparedness page documents the Emergency Operations Plan (rev. March 2021) and a Kuali-based Business Continuity Plans program administered by Public Safety; unit BC plans themselves are internal.

framework_alignment: Business Continuity Management Program with four disciplines (emergency preparedness, crisis management, IT DR, business resumption)
evidence_quote: The objective of a business continuity management program is to develop and implement plans that ensure expeditious response, continuity, and recovery of critical business functions or services during and after an incident. ... Business continuity plans shall be developed and documented for the critical business functions identified in the business impact analysis conducted throughout the University.
catalog_notes: Comprehensive Business Continuity Management Policy spanning four disciplines. Requires departmental Business Impact Analysis (life-safety/financial/reputation/compliance/productivity factors), documented response/recovery/resumption plans, annual testing, and disaster recovery plans with defined timeframes. Plans housed in approved content management system.

framework_alignment: FEMA, NYSOEM, NIMS/ICS; all-hazards risk-based program
evidence_quote: The CEMP guides overall policy and procedures for actions related to emergency planning, preparedness, response and recovery including business continuity and business recovery planning. The CEMP identifies Portfolios, Departments and individuals responsible for emergency planning, emergency response, business continuity and business recovery planning.
catalog_notes: Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan adheres to four principles including 'Continuity of essential functions' and 'Restoration of campus services and activities,' and incorporates an annual Hazard Vulnerability Analysis. Full CEMP held securely by the Director of Public Safety and Emergency Management; the public page describes its scope and business continuity content.

evidence_quote: Creating a continuity plan is essential for your unit or office. Having a plan helps your office continue its business uninterrupted after an emergency or disaster. ... All Univeristy departments on all campuses are required to develop Business Continuity Plan(s) for their offices.
catalog_notes: University Police and Public Safety continuity planning page; all Penn State departments on all campuses are required to develop Business Continuity Plans, supported by the Emergency Management Office. Penn State also publishes a Comprehensive Emergency Management Plan (CEMP).
